meta.name: Strong's Greek (partial)
meta.note: Starter lexicon — ~50 high-frequency entries. Full ~5624-entry corpus is a follow-up.

G25.def: perhaps from ἄγαν (much) (or compare H5689); to love (in a social or moral sense).

G26.def: from G25; love, i.e. affection or benevolence; specially (plural) a love-feast.

G40.def: from ἅγος (an awful thing) (compare G53, G2282); sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated).

G281.def: of Hebrew origin (H543); properly, firm, i.e., (figuratively) trustworthy; adverbially, surely (often as interjection, so be it).

G444.def: from G435 and ὤψ; man-faced, i.e., a human being.

G652.def: from G649; a delegate; specially, an ambassador of the Gospel; officially a commissioner of Christ.

G746.def: from G756; (properly abstract) a commencement, or (concretely) chief (in various applications of order, time, place, or rank).

G932.def: from G935; properly, royalty, i.e., (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm.

G1093.def: contracted from a primary word; soil; by extension a region, or the solid part or the whole of the terrene globe.

G1096.def: a prolongation and middle voice form of a primary verb; to cause to be (generate), i.e., (reflexively) to become (come into being).

G1097.def: a prolonged form of a primary verb; to know (absolutely) in a great variety of applications and with many implications.

G1242.def: from G1303; properly, a disposition, i.e., (specially) a contract (especially a devisory will).

G1343.def: from G1342; equity (of character or act); specially (Christian) justification.

G1391.def: from the base of G1380; glory (as very apparent), in a wide application (literal or figurative, objective or subjective).

G1411.def: from G1410; force (literally or figuratively); specially, miraculous power (usually by implication, a miracle itself).

G1515.def: probably from a primary verb εἴρω (to join); peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity.

G1577.def: from a compound of G1537 and a derivative of G2564; a calling out, i.e., (concretely) a popular meeting, especially a religious congregation.

G1680.def: from a primary ἔλπω (to anticipate, usually with pleasure); expectation (abstractly or concretely) or confidence.

G1722.def: a primary preposition denoting (fixed) position (in place, time or state), and (by implication) instrumentality.

G2288.def: from G2348; (properly, an adjective used as a noun) death (literally or figuratively).

G2316.def: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588) the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.

G2424.def: of Hebrew origin (H3091); Jesus (i.e., Jehoshua), the name of our Lord and two (three) other Israelites.

G2588.def: prolonged from a primary κάρ (Latin cor, 'heart'); the heart, i.e., (figuratively) the thoughts or feelings (mind); also (by analogy) the middle.

G2889.def: probably from the base of G2865; orderly arrangement, i.e., decoration; by implication, the world (in a wide or narrow sense, including its inhabitants).

G2962.def: from κῦρος (supremacy); supreme in authority, i.e., (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title).

G3056.def: from G3004; something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty).

G3340.def: from G3326 and G3539; to think differently or afterwards, i.e., reconsider (morally, feel compunction).

G3439.def: from G3441 and G1096; only-born, i.e., sole.

G3551.def: from a primary νέμω (to parcel out, especially food or grazing to animals); law (through the idea of prescriptive usage).

G3624.def: of uncertain affinity; a dwelling (more or less extensive, literal or figurative); by implication, a family.

G3686.def: from a presumed derivative of the base of G1097 (compare G3685); a 'name' (literally or figuratively).

G3704.def: from G3739 and G4459; what(-ever) how, i.e., in the manner that (as adverb or conjunction of coincidence, intentional or actual).

G3962.def: apparently a primary word; a 'father' (literally or figuratively, near or more remote).

G4100.def: from G4102; to have faith (in, upon, or with respect to, a person or thing), i.e., credit; by implication, to entrust.

G4102.def: from G3982; persuasion, i.e., credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher).

G4151.def: from G4154; a current of air, i.e., breath (blast) or a breeze; by analogy or figuratively, a spirit, i.e., (human) the rational soul, (by implication) vital principle, mental disposition, etc., or (superhuman) an angel, demon, or (divine) God.

G4678.def: from G4680; wisdom (higher or lower, worldly or spiritual).

G4982.def: from a primary σῶς (contraction for obsolete σάος, 'safe'); to save, i.e., deliver or protect (literally or figuratively).

G4991.def: feminine of a derivative of G4990 as (properly, abstract) noun; rescue or safety (physically or morally).

G5207.def: apparently a primary word; a 'son' (sometimes of animals), used very widely of immediate, remote or figuratively, kinship.

G5368.gloss: to love (as a friend), to kiss
G5368.def: from G5384; to be a friend to (fond of an individual or an object), i.e., have affection for.

G5384.def: properly, dear, i.e., a friend; actively, fond, i.e., friendly (still as a noun, an associate, neighbor, etc.).

G5457.def: from an obsolete φάω (to shine or make manifest, especially by rays); luminousness (in the widest application, natural or artificial, abstract or concrete, literal or figurative).

G5485.def: from G5463; graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude).

G5547.def: from G5548; anointed, i.e., the Messiah, an epithet of Jesus.

G5590.def: from G5594; breath, i.e., (by implication) spirit, abstractly or concretely (the animal sentient principle only; thus distinguished on the one hand from G4151, which is the rational and immortal soul; and on the other from G2222, which is mere vitality, even of plants).

G5610.def: apparently a primary word; an 'hour' (literally or figuratively).
